item.title: React 中的动画与 Framer Motion | Animations in React with Framer Motion
item.description:
当像 Framer Motion 这样的工具可用时，为什么要编写多行 CSS 代码来创建动画？ 许多开发人员避免将动画添加到他们的 Web 应用程序，因为动画代码编写起来可能很严格。 但是，使用 Framer Motion，您只需几行代码即可执行动画。 在本文中，我们将通过使用 Framer Motion 为文本和图像制作动画来检查 Framer Motion 的功能、安装过程和可用性。

我们将介绍的步骤：

先决条件

要学习本教程，您需要：

  • 清楚了解基本的前端技术，即 HTML、CSS 和 JavaScript。
  • 已安装节点 Js。

什么是成帧器运动？

要使用 Framer Motion，您需要安装该库并将其集成到您的 React 项目中。

使用以下命令安装 Framer 运动。

 npm install framer-motion

下一步是将您希望使用的 Framer Motion 组件导入您的 React 应用程序。

笔记：
我们将把这篇文章分成两部分，首先是文本动画，其次是图像动画。 最后，我们会将两者结合到一个 React 应用程序中。

文字动画入门

在本节中，我们将演示如何在使用motion组件为文本制作动画时使用 Framer Motion。

将以下代码添加到您的App.js文件中。

 import { motion } from " framer-motion " ; function App () { return ( < div className = " App " > < motion . h1 animate = {{ x : [ 50 , 150 , 50 ], opacity : 1 , scale : 1 }} transition = {{ duration : 5 , delay : 0.3 , ease : [ 0.5 , 0.71 , 1 , 1.5 ], }} initial = {{ opacity : 0 , scale : 0.5 }} whileHover = {{ scale : 1.2 }} > Animation made easy with Framer Motion < /motion.h1 > < /div > ); } export default App ;

我们导入了 Framer Motion 的motion组件。 仔细观察h1元素，您会发现它与标准h1元素有何不同。

使用 Framer Motion 制作动画的第一步是使用motion组件，它可以与任何标准 HTML 元素一起使用。

我们将通过使用h1中的motion组件和 Framer Motion animate道具来完成动画，为我们的文本提供一个很酷的入口动画，以便在页面重新加载时逐渐显示。

通过这种方法，我们可以为文本设置动画来满足我们的愿望。 首先，我们为文本分配一个位置，指示动画将在何处发生。 然后我们将在 x 轴上为我们的文本设置动画。 您可以将x设置为[0, 150, 50]的值。

接下来，让我们给它的opacity为 1。

我们需要为酷炫的进入动画分配一个比例。 我们不想要任何狂野的东西。 我们可以将比例设置为 1。所以当文本动画达到比例 1 时，它会停止增加大小。

最后，我们使用 Framer Motion transition道具，因为每个有效的动画都需要一个过渡过程。 我们将在这个道具中指定我们的文本动画的duration 、 delay和ease 。 给它 5 的持续时间、3 的延迟和[0.5, 0.71, 1, 1.01]的缓和度。

缓动显示文本动画在进入其原始比例之前进入的比例。

但是，我们不想停留在文本动画中的酷入口。 让我们为我们的文本添加一个漂亮的悬停效果。 当你的鼠标悬停在它上面时，它会出现反弹或变大。

为此，我们将使用whileHover并将其缩放到 1.2。

 function App () { return ( < div className = " App " > < motion . h1 ... // ====> whileHover = {{ scale : 1.2 }} // <==== > Animation made easy with Framer Motion < /motion.h1 > < /div > ); }

图像动画入门

在本节中，我们将演示如何在动画图像时使用 Framer Motion。

首先，您需要在“src”文件夹中创建一个图像文件夹来保存您的图像。 为了这个项目，我使用了一些随机图像。 随意使用您选择的任何图像。

在“src”文件夹中创建一个“image.js”文件，并在其中添加以下代码：

 import fox from " ./images/pexels-fox-1071249.jpg " ; import jill from " ./images/jill.jpg " ; import cha1 from " ./images/cha1.jpg " ; import cha2 from " ./images/cha2.jpg " ; export default [ fox , jill , cha1 , cha2 ]

在我们的 App.js 文件中使用它们之前，我们App.js image.js中。 这将帮助我们避免在我们将使用这些图像的地方进行冗余导入。

将以下代码添加到您的App.css文件中。

 .item { min-height : 10rem ; min-width : 30rem ; padding : 5px ; } .item img { width : 60% ; height : 50% ; border-radius : 1rem ; pointer-events : none ; padding : 15% ; } .inner-carousel { display : flex ; background : rgb ( 76 , 76 , 76 ); height : 23rem ; } .carousel { cursor : grab ; overflow : hidden ; background : rgb ( 215 , 216 , 215 ); height : 23rem ; }

使用以下代码更改App.js文件：

 import { motion } from " framer-motion " ; import " ./App.css " ; import images from " ./image " ; function App () { return ( < div className = " App " > < motion . div className = " carousel " > < motion . div animate = {{ scale : [ 1 , 1 , 1 , 1 , 1 ], rotate : [ 0 , 30 , 60 , 240 , 360 ], }} > { images . map (( image ) => { return ( < motion . div whileHover = {{ scale : 1.2 }} whileTap = {{ scale : 2 }} className = " item " transition = {{ duration : 3 }} key = { image } > < img src = { image } alt = " image " /> < /motion.div > ); })} < /motion.div > < /motion.div > < /div > ); } export default App ;

图像动画将采用轮播的形式，每次刷新页面时具有旋转效果，每次将鼠标移近时会产生悬停效果，每次触摸图像时都会产生点击效果。

对于旋转木马，创建三个容器，一个用于外部旋转木马，一个用于内部旋转木马，一个用于图像。 所有三个容器都应使用 Framer Motion motion组件创建，一个在另一个内部。

内部旋转木马将执行旋转和滑动效果。 此时将调用 framer motion animate道具，缩放到[1,1,1,1,1]并旋转到[0,30, 60, 240, 360] 。

这将赋予它独特的旋转效果，如下所示：

我们将使用dragConstraint道具和 framer 运动drag来给它一种类似旋转木马的感觉。 要设置一个停止器，请使用dragConstraint道具。 为了让我们的旋转木马向左移动然后返回到之前的位置，我们设置了dragConstraint:{ right: 0, left: -1100 } 。

仅使用drag prop和dragConstraint道具移动似乎太无聊了； 相反，让我们在拖动的每一端添加一个类似弹跳的效果。 这可以通过 Framer Motion 的drag transition道具来实现。

我们将利用“bounceStiffner”和“bounceDamping”来实现drag transition属性。 分别给出 600 和 8 的bounceStiffner和bounceDamping值。 最后，我们将在这个容器中浏览我们导入的图像，并将每个图像添加回轮播。

将突出显示的代码添加到App.js

 function App () { return ( < div className = " App " > < motion . div className = " carousel " > < motion . div ... // highlight-start drag = " x " dragConstraints = {{ right : 0 , left : - 1100 }} dragTransition = {{ bounceStiffness : 600 , bounceDamping : 8 }} // highlight-end > ... < /motion.div > < /motion.div > < /div > ); }

轮播中的最后一个div将容纳返回的图像。 为了在这个div,我们将使用帧器运动whileHover 、 whileTap和一个过渡道具。

使用比例为 1.2 的 framer 运动whileHover为我们的图像提供一个很好的悬停效果。 当您的鼠标悬停在图像上时，它只会放大到这个最大值。

然后，通过给whileTap赋予 2 的比例，我们将给我们的图像一个点击效果，以便当我们点击它们时它们会反弹。 我们必须定义一个过渡持续时间，以便悬停和点击变得更具吸引力。 让我们选择 3 秒作为持续时间。

 function App () { return ( < div className = " App " > < motion . div className = " carousel " > < motion . div > { images . map (( image ) => { return ( < motion . div ... // ====> whileHover = {{ scale : 1.2 }} whileTap = {{ scale : 2 }} // <==== > < img src = { image } alt = " image " /> < /motion.div > ); })} < /motion.div > < /motion.div > < /div > ); }

与普通的 CSS 不同，我们可以看到 framer motion 可以快速创建上述动画，并且只需要几行代码。 一些程序员喜欢使用他们自己的动画和原始 CSS，但如果你像我一样喜欢更简单的解决方案，你可以使用 Framer Motion 来制作你的动画。

您可以在此处访问该应用程序

结论

本文教我们如何使用 Framer Motion 为 React 应用程序创建简单的文本和图像动画。 与常规 CSS 相比，我们看到用几行代码实现这些动画是多么容易。 我们还看到了如何使用一些 Framer Motion 组件和道具。 你应该检查出来。 我希望这篇文章对你有价值。

无限制地构建基于 React 的 CRUD 应用程序

现代 CRUD 应用程序需要使用来自许多不同来源的数据，从自定义 API 到 Supabase、Hasura、Airtable 和 Strapi 等后端服务。

如果您对后端无关、无头框架感兴趣，请查看细化，该框架可以通过内置提供程序和社区插件连接 15 多个数据源。



精炼是一个基于 React 的开源框架，用于无限制地构建 CRUD 应用程序。
它可以将您的开发时间加快3 倍，而不会影响样式、自定义和项目工作流程的自由度。

精炼在设计上是无头的，它连接了30 多个开箱即用的后端服务，包括自定义 REST 和 GraphQL API。

访问优化 GitHub 存储库以获取更多信息、演示、教程和示例项目。



原文:

When a tool like Framer Motion is available, why write many lines of CSS code to create an animation? Web applications look more exciting and beautiful when they are animated.

Many developers avoid adding animation to their web applications since animation codes can be rigorous to write. However, with Framer Motion, you can execute animations with only a few lines of code. This external library in React.js makes animation incredibly simple, allowing the developer to concentrate on other aspects of the project.

In this article, we'll examine Framer Motion's functionality, installation process, and usability by using it to animate text and images.

Steps we'll cover:

Prerequisites

To follow along with this tutorial, you need to have:

  • A clear understanding of basic frontend technologies, i.e., HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.
  • Knowledge of React.JS
  • Node Js installed.

What is Framer Motion?

Framer motion is a powerful production-ready library from Framer that can create different animation styles in the DOM as elements. Framer Motion is an excellent alternative to making complex CSS animations. To use Framer Motion, you need to install the library and integrate it into your React project.

Getting started with Framer Motion in React

React requires that we install any external libraries using the npm package before utilizing them. Install Framer motion with the following command.

npm install framer-motion

The next step is to import the Framer Motion component you wish to use into your React app.

Note:
We are going to break this article into two sections first, the text animation, and second, the image animation. In the end, we will combine both into one React application.

Getting started with text animation

In this section, we'll demonstrate how to use Framer Motion when animating text using the motion components.

Add the following code to your App.js file.

import { motion } from "framer-motion"; function App() { return ( <div className="App"> <motion.h1 animate={{ x: [50, 150, 50], opacity: 1, scale: 1 }} transition={{ duration: 5, delay: 0.3, ease: [0.5, 0.71, 1, 1.5], }} initial={{ opacity: 0, scale: 0.5 }} whileHover={{ scale: 1.2 }} > Animation made easy with Framer Motion </motion.h1>
 </div>
 );
}
export default App;

We imported Framer Motion's motion component. Look closely at the h1 element you will notice how it differs from the standard h1 element.

The first step in using Framer Motion to animate is to use the motion component, which can be used with any standard HTML element.

We will give our text a cool entry animation to show gradually when the page reloads by utilizing the motion component in our h1 and a Framer Motion animate prop to accomplish the animation.

With this approach, we can animate our text to fulfill our desires. First, we assign a position to our text, indicating where the animation will take place. Then we will animate our text on the x-axis. You can set x to have a value of [0, 150, 50].

Next, let's give it an opacity of 1.

We need to assign a scale to the cool entering animation. We don't want anything wild. We can set the scale to 1. So when the text animation reaches scale 1, it stops increasing in size.

Finally, we use the Framer Motion transition prop because every effective animation requires a transition process. We will specify the duration, delay, and ease of our text animation inside this prop. Give it a duration of 5, a delay of 3, and an ease of [0.5, 0.71, 1, 1.01].

The easing shows the scale at which the text animation enters before going to its original scale.

However, we don't want to stop with the cool entrance in our text animation. Let's add a beautiful hover effect to our text. When your mouse hovers over it, it will appear to bounce or grow bigger in size.

To accomplish this, we'll use the whileHover prop and scale it to 1.2.

function App() { return ( <div className="App"> <motion.h1 ... // ====> whileHover={{ scale: 1.2 }} // <==== > Animation made easy with Framer Motion </motion.h1>
 </div>
 );
}



Getting started with image animation

In this section, we'll demonstrate how to use Framer Motion when animating images.

First, you need to create an image folder inside the "src" folder to keep your images. For the sake of this project, I used some random images. Feel free to use any image of your choice.

Create an “image.js” file inside the “src” folder and add the following code inside:

import fox from "./images/pexels-fox-1071249.jpg";
import jill from "./images/jill.jpg";
import cha1 from "./images/cha1.jpg";
import cha2 from "./images/cha2.jpg"; export default[fox, jill, cha1,cha2]

We are going to import all our images into the image.js file mentioned above before using them in our App.js file. This will help us avoid redundant importation where we will use these images.

Add the following code to your App.css file.

.item{ min-height: 10rem; min-width: 30rem; padding: 5px;
} .item img{ width: 60%; height: 50%; border-radius: 1rem; pointer-events: none; padding: 15%;
} .inner-carousel { display: flex; background: rgb(76, 76, 76); height: 23rem;
} .carousel{ cursor: grab; overflow: hidden; background: rgb(215, 216, 215); height: 23rem;
}

Change App.js file with the following code:

import { motion } from "framer-motion";
import "./App.css";
import images from "./image"; function App() { return ( <div className="App"> <motion.div className="carousel"> <motion.div animate={{ scale: [1, 1, 1, 1, 1], rotate: [0, 30, 60, 240, 360], }} > {images.map((image) => { return ( <motion.div whileHover={{ scale: 1.2 }} whileTap={{ scale: 2 }} className="item" transition={{ duration: 3 }} key={image} > <img src={image} alt="image" /> </motion.div>
 ); })} </motion.div>
 </motion.div>
 </div>
 );
} export default App;

The image animation will take the form of a carousel with spinning effects each time the page is refreshed, hover effects each time the mouse is moved close to them, and tap effects each time the image is touched.

For the carousel, create three containers, one for the outer carousel, one for the inner carousel, and one for the images. All three containers should be created using Framer Motion motion components, one inside the other.

The inner carousel will perform the spinning and sliding effects. The framer motion animate prop will be called in at this point, scaled to [1,1,1,1,1], and rotated to [0,30, 60, 240, 360].

This will give it a unique spinning effect, as you can see below:

We'll use dragConstraint prop and framer motion drag to give it a carousel-like feel. “X” will be passed as a parameter in the drag prop because we need our carousel to move along the x-axis.

To set a stopper in place, utilize the dragConstraint prop. For our carousel to travel to the left and then return to its previous location, we set the dragConstraint:{ right: 0, left: -1100 }.

It appears too boring to move with just the drag prop and dragConstraint props; instead, let's add a bounce-like effect at each end of the drag. This can be achieved with the drag transition props from Framer Motion.

We'll take advantage of the "bounceStiffner" and "bounceDamping" for drag transition attributes. Give the bounceStiffner and bounceDamping values of 600 and 8, respectively. Finally, we will navigate through our imported images in this container and add each image back to the carousel.

Add highlighted code to App.js

function App() { return ( <div className="App"> <motion.div className="carousel"> <motion.div ... // highlight-start drag="x" dragConstraints={{right: 0, left:-1100}} dragTransition={{ bounceStiffness: 600, bounceDamping: 8 }} // highlight-end > ... </motion.div>
 </motion.div>
 </div>
 );
}

The last div in the carousel will house the returned images. To create the appropriate animation inside this div, we will use the framer motions whileHover, whileTap, and a transition prop.

Use the framer motion whileHover with a scale of 1.2 to give our image a nice hover effect. When your mouse is over an image, it will only scale up to this maximum.

Then, by giving the whileTap prop a scale of 2, we will give our images a tap effect so that they bounce when we tap on them. We must define a transition duration so that hovering and tapping become more engaging. Let's choose 3 seconds as the duration.

function App() { return ( <div className="App"> <motion.div className="carousel"> <motion.div> {images.map((image) => { return ( <motion.div ... // ====> whileHover={{ scale: 1.2 }} whileTap={{ scale: 2 }} // <====  > <img src={image} alt="image" /> </motion.div>
 ); })} </motion.div>
 </motion.div>
 </div>
 );
}

Contrary to ordinary CSS, we can see that framer motion enabled the above animation to be created quickly and with a few lines of code. Some programmers like to use their own animation with raw CSS, but if you're like me and prefer a simpler solution, you can use Framer Motion for your animation.

You can access the app here

Conclusion

This article taught us how to use Framer Motion to create a simple text and image animation for a React application. Compared to regular CSS, we saw how easy it is to implement these animations with a few lines of code. We also saw how to use some Framer Motion components and props. Framer Motion has other powerful features we didn't cover in this article. You should check them out. I hope this article becomes valuable to you.

*Writer: Joel Ezimorah *



Build your React-based CRUD applications without constraints

Modern CRUD applications are required to consume data from many different sources from custom API’s to backend services like Supabase, Hasura, Airtable and Strapi.

Check out refine, if you are interested in a backend agnostic, headless framework which can connect 15+ data sources thanks to built-in providers and community plugins.



refine is an open-source React-based framework for building CRUD applications without constraints.
It can speed up your development time up to 3X without compromising freedom on styling, customization and project workflow.

refine is headless by design and it connects 30+ backend services out-of-the-box including custom REST and GraphQL API’s.

Visit refine GitHub repository for more information, demos, tutorials, and example projects.
item.author: necatiozmen

item.title: 面向 Web 开发人员的超棒 GitHub 存储库 - 第 2 部分 | Awesome GitHub Repositories for Web Developer - Part 2
item.description:
在上一篇文章中，我们了解了 5 个面向 Web 开发人员的 GitHub 存储库。 我们回来了最新的部分，以提供更多这样的存储库，以帮助您在 Web 开发领域中成长。

在本系列中，即 Awesome GitHub Repositories，我们正在讨论一些可以增强您的知识的存储库。 我们还将在接下来的部分中包含 React、CSS、Web3 等的存储库。 因此，您可以在 Twitter ( @surajondev ) 上关注我以了解最新帖子。

让我们从一些很棒的存储库开始。

该存储库比较了编写 JavaScript 代码时的坏习惯和好习惯。

它有一些提示，包括：

  • 变量
  • 功能
  • 对象和数据结构
  • 课程
  • 和其他 JS 概念

阳顺/科技面试手册

使用这本很棒的面试手册申请并准备面试。 忙碌的开发人员可以使用此存储库为即将到来的面试做好准备。 它有一个从准备简历到破解面试的分步指南。 该指南采用文档形式，可帮助开发人员轻松理解本书的结构。

它有以下主题：

  • 学习与实践计划
  • 解决问题的技巧
  • 模拟编码面试
  • 编码面试
  • 还有很多

您可以将本指南用于任何编程语言。

你不需要/你不需要

人们选择受欢迎的项目，通常不是因为它适用于他们的问题

存储库的资源以 You don't need X 开头，例如 You Don't Need Loops。 这是一个有趣的存储库，可以了解某些概念/工具是否是必要的，或者您只是因为其他人正在使用它们而使用它们。

开发 Web 应用程序前端时要勾选的清单，以使其在技术性和 SEO 方面更好。 强烈建议将其中一些复选框用于您的 Web 应用程序。

回购有不同的复选框：

  • 头
  • 社交媒体
  • 网页字体
  • 和别的

通过从头开始重新创建您最喜欢的技术来掌握编程。

一个存储库，具有专门用于构建您自己的 X 的资源，例如构建您自己的 React。 从 Web 开发到使用 C 进行游戏，各种资源是一个加分点。

一些主题包括：

  • 机器人
  • 游戏
  • 网络服务器
  • 前端框架/库
  • 和别的

与我联系

我们为 Web 开发人员浏览了一些有趣且有用的 GitHub 存储库。 您可以使用这些存储库来学习和掌握 Web 开发。

我希望这篇文章达到了它的目的。 关注我以获取未来的部分和其他内容。 感谢您阅读博文。



原文:

We have learned about 5 GitHub repositories for Web Developers in the previous article. We are back with the latest part to provide more such repositories to help you grow in the web development domain.

Web development is simple but finding the right resources and path will help you break into it quite easily. In this series i.e, Awesome GitHub Repositories, we are discussing about some repositories that will enhance your knowledge. We will also be including repositories for React, CSS, Web3, and much more in the upcoming parts. So, you can follow me on Twitter(@surajondev) to know about the latest posts.

Let's get started with some awesome repositories.

ryanmcdermott/clean-code-javascript

🛁 Clean Code concepts adapted for JavaScript

Robert C. Martin's book Clean Code adopted in Javascript to provides you with some tips to improve the readability of your code. The repository has compared the bad and good practices while writing JavaScript code.

It has tips including:

  • Variables
  • Functions
  • Objects and Data Structures
  • Classes
  • And other JS concepts

yangshun/tech-interview-handbook

💯 Curated coding interview preparation materials for busy software engineers

Apply and prepare for an interview with this awesome interview handbook. Busy developers can use this repository to prepare themselves for the upcoming interview. It has a step-by-step guide from preparing a resume to cracking the Interview. The guide is in the form of documentation which will help developers to understand the structure of the book easily.

It has topics such as:

  • Study and Practice Plan
  • Technique to Solve Questions
  • Mock Coding Interview
  • Coding Interview
  • And many more

You can use this guide for any programming language.

you-dont-need/You-Dont-Need

People choose popular projects, often not because it applies to their problems

The repository has resources that start with You don't need X for instance You Don't Need Loops. It's an interesting repository to understand whether some concepts/tools are necessary or that you just using them because others are using them.

thedaviddias/Front-End-Checklist

🗂 The perfect Front-End Checklist for modern websites and meticulous developers

A checklist to tick when developing the frontend of your web application to make it better in terms of technicality and SEO. Some of these checkboxes are highly recommended for your web application.

Repo has different checkboxes for:

  • Head
  • HTML tags
  • Social Media
  • Web Fonts
  • And others

codecrafters-io/build-your-own-x

Master programming by recreating your favorite technologies from scratch.

A repository having resources dedicated to building your own X for instance Build your own React. The variety of resources is a plus point from web development to gaming with C.

Some of the topics included:

  • Bot
  • Game
  • Web Server
  • Front-end Framework / Library
  • And others

Connect With Me

We have gone through some of the interesting and useful GitHub repositories for web developers. You can use these repositories to learn and ace the web development.

I hope this article has served its purpose. Follow me for future parts and other content. Thanks for reading the blog post.
item.author: surajondev

item.title: 宣布 Appwrite 的 Flutter 和 Dart SDK 的新版本 | Announcing New Versions for Appwrite's Flutter and Dart SDKs
item.description:
我们很高兴地宣布推出适用于 Flutter 和 Dart 的 Appwrite SDK 7.0 和 6.1 版。 新版本添加了一些修复并更新了两个 SDK 的过时依赖项。

我们也很高兴分享 Flutter SDK 的 7.0 版现在完全支持 Windows 应用程序的 OAuth 身份验证。 如果您已经在 Android 设备的早期版本的 Appwrite SDK 中使用 OAuth 身份验证，则需要确保使用新设置更新AndroidManifest.xml清单文件：

 <manifest ... > ... <application ... > ... <!-- Add this inside the `<application>` tag, along side the existing `<activity>` tags --> <activity android:name= "com.linusu.flutter_web_auth_2.CallbackActivity" android:exported= "true" > <intent-filter android:label= "flutter_web_auth_2" > <action android:name= "android.intent.action.VIEW" /> <category android:name= "android.intent.category.DEFAULT" /> <category android:name= "android.intent.category.BROWSABLE" /> <data android:scheme= "appwrite-callback-[PROJECT_ID]" /> </intent-filter> </activity> </application> </manifest>

如果您将包与 Web 应用程序一起使用，则需要更新回调文件，如下例所示：

 <!DOCTYPE html> <title> Authentication complete </title> <p> Authentication is complete. If this does not happen automatically, please close the window. <script> window . opener . postMessage ({ ' flutter-web-auth2 ' : window . location . href }, window . location . origin ); window . close (); </script>

如您所见，更改很小，意味着 Appwrite 现在使用的是flutter_web_auth_2包而不是flutter_web_auth 。 新包包含多项改进和错误修复，应该可以在使用 Appwrite SDK for Flutter 时提供更好的体验。

这也是感谢Nico Mexis分叉和维护新的flutter_web_auth_2包以及许多 Appwrite 社区成员的好机会，包括Dom Jocubeit 、 Bishwajeet Parhi 、 Agon Noga和Kamil Pyrkosz提出、讨论和帮助解决这些问题。

学到更多

如果您是 Appwrite 的新手，Appwrite 是一个完全 100% 开源的后端即服务平台，为开发人员提供构建 Web 或移动应用程序所需的所有核心 API。 您可以通过访问Appwrite 文档了解有关Appwrite的更多信息并开始使用Appwrite SDK 。

一旦暂停，appwrite 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，appwrite 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，appwrite 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 appwrite 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 appwrite 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 appwrite 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

We're happy to announce the release of versions 7.0 and 6.1 of the Appwrite SDKs for Flutter and Dart. The new versions add some fixes and update outdated dependencies for both SDKs.

We're also happy to share that version 7.0 of the Flutter SDKs now fully supports OAuth authentication for Windows applications. If you're already using OAuth authentication with previous versions of the Appwrite SDK with Android devices, you will need to make sure you update the AndroidManifest.xml manifest file with the new settings:

<manifest ...> ... <application ...> ... <!-- Add this inside the `<application>` tag, along side the existing `<activity>` tags --> <activity android:name="com.linusu.flutter_web_auth_2.CallbackActivity" android:exported="true"> <intent-filter android:label="flutter_web_auth_2"> <action android:name="android.intent.action.VIEW" /> <category android:name="android.intent.category.DEFAULT" /> <category android:name="android.intent.category.BROWSABLE" /> <data android:scheme="appwrite-callback-[PROJECT_ID]" /> </intent-filter> </activity> </application>
</manifest>

In case you're using the package with web applications, you will need to update your callback file like the following example:

<!DOCTYPE html>
<title>Authentication complete</title>
<p>Authentication is complete. If this does not happen automatically, please
close the window.
<script> window.opener.postMessage({ 'flutter-web-auth2': window.location.href }, window.location.origin); window.close();
</script>

As you can see, the change is minor and meant to indicate that Appwrite is now using the flutter_web_auth_2 package instead of flutter_web_auth. The new package includes multiple improvements and bug fixes that should enable a better experience when using the Appwrite SDK for Flutter.

This is also a great opportunity to thank Nico Mexis for forking and maintaining the new flutter_web_auth_2 package and to many of the Appwrite community members, including Dom Jocubeit, Bishwajeet Parhi, Agon Noga and Kamil Pyrkosz for raising, discussing and helping address those issues.

Learn More

If you're new to Appwrite, Appwrite is a fully 100% open source backend as a service platform providing developers with all the core APIs required to build Web or Mobile applications. You can learn more about Appwrite and get started with the Appwrite SDK by visiting the Appwrite Documentation.

🌚 Enable dark mode🔠 Change your default font

📚 Adjust your experience level to see more relevant contentA

InterSystems Developer - Jul 24

Rukshan J. Senanayaka - Aug 15

Once suspended, appwrite will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, appwrite will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by appwrite will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If appwrite is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Thanks for keeping DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻 safe. Here is what you can do to flag appwrite:

appwrite consistently posts content that violates DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging appwrite will restore default visibility to their posts.

item.title: 关于 Github Copilot 你需要知道的一切！！！ | Everything You Need To Know About Github Copilot!!!
item.description:
德瓦希·辛皮

发表于不久前，人工智能还是科幻电影的主要对手。 而今天，它就在我们身边。 几乎所有来自科技世界的产品似乎都带有某种人工智能元素。 人工智能对我们的日常生活产生了重大影响，但你有没有想过人工智能的真正能力是什么？ 未来愿景成为现实的一个很好的例子是GitHub Copilot 。

您唯一需要做的就是输入函数名称或一些注释，Copilot 将自动填写实现。 该工具在云中处理用户的输入并返回一个片段，您只需接受、拒绝或要求进一步的解决方案建议。

但是，价格是多少？

然而，注册GitHub 全球校园计划的学生和流行的开源 GitHub 项目的维护者——当用户导航到 GitHub Copilot 订阅页面时被识别——仍然可以免费使用该工具。

在进入程序员市场之后，人们广泛讨论它是朝着计算机编程的消亡迈出的一大步，还是只是另一种类固醇的自动完成工具。 好吧，它仍然需要一些严肃的知识来构建软件。 即使有 Copilot 的帮助，您也需要知道自己在做什么、验证和理解生成的代码。 非程序员（目前）不可能跳上 Copilot 并构建他们想要的任何东西。

该工具并不完美。 它并不总是生成正确的代码。 那里有很多不好的做法和不推荐使用的代码。 更糟糕的是，Copilot 可以编写安全漏洞，尤其是在内存不安全的语言中。 它还不是 100% 可靠的。 您绝对需要查看 Copilot 的代码。 这就是人工智能。 人工智能需要学习。 随着时间的推移它会变得更好，但我们需要等待。

结论

事实上，过度依赖这样的工具可能会导致不必要的工作，甚至是严重的问题。

感谢您阅读到这里。 同时，您可以查看我的其他博客文章并访问我的Github 。

我目前正在研究Stone CSS ( Github )

一旦暂停，devarshishimpi 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，devarshishimpi 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发布，devarshishimpi 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 devarshishimpi 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 devarshishimpi 的方法：

原文:

Devarshi Shimpi

Posted on Sep 1

Not so long ago, artificial intelligence was the main antagonist of Sci-Fi movies. And today, it is all around us. Almost every product that comes out of the technology world seems to have some element of artificial intelligence. AI has a significant impact on our daily life, but have you ever wondered what AI is really capable of? A great example of a futuristic vision becoming reality is the GitHub Copilot.

GitHub Copilot is super cool, but what is it?

Copilot is a tool built into the code editor that is capable of writing code itself based on the code you’ve already written in your project. The only thing you need to do is type a function name or some comments and Copilot will automatically fill in the implementation. The tool processes the user’s input in the cloud and comes back with a snippet that you just accept, decline, or ask for further solution suggestions.

But, What's the Price?

GitHub Copilot licenses, which are currently only available to individual users, cost $10 per month or $100 per year. However, students enrolled in GitHub's Global Campus Program and maintainers of popular open source GitHub projects -- identified when a user navigates to the GitHub Copilot subscription page -- will still be able to use the tool for free.

Can GitHub Copilot be any threat to developers?

After hitting the programmer’s market, it has been widely discussed whether it is a big step toward the death of computer programming or just another autocomplete tool on steroids. Well, it still requires some serious knowledge to build software. Even with Copilot’s help, you need to know what you are doing, verify and understand generated code. It’s not possible (yet) for non-programmers to jump on Copilot and build whatever they want.

The tool is not perfect. It doesn’t always generate the correct code. There are a lot of bad practices and deprecated code out there. Even worse, Copilot can write security vulnerabilities, especially in memory unsafe languages. It isn’t 100% reliable yet. You absolutely need to review Copilot’s code. This is AI. AI needs to learn. It will get better with time, but we need to wait.

Conclusion

Copilot is just one of many tools that improve and will keep improving our work. In fact, relying too much on tools like that might lead to unnecessary work or even serious problems.

Thank You for reading till here. Meanwhile you can check out my other blog posts and visit my Github.

I am currently working on Stone CSS ( Github )

item.title: 关于 GitHub README 的注释 | Notes on GitHub READMEs
item.description:
我第一次看到“README”这个词时，我正试图完成一项任务。 指令是创建一个 README.md 文件； 添加一些关于项目的信息，并将其推送到我的 GitHub。 直到我不得不从事一个需要部署的协作项目时，我才完全理解它的目的。

编码最佳实践要求每个存储库都有一个 README 文件。

什么是自述文件？

自述文件是一个文本文件，它为用户提供有关您项目的基本所需信息，一目了然。 它描述了您的项目，说明您的项目为何有价值，用户可以对您的项目做什么，以及他们如何使用它等等。 当人们检查您在存储库中的任何项目时，这通常是他们参与的第一件事。 它需要简洁。

项目描述：

正确起草的描述包括以下内容： 您的项目名称； 湾。 你的项目是做什么的； 目标用户是谁； 这个怎么运作; 和

您使用的技术以及使用它们的原因

项目描述允许其他开发人员和合作者对您的项目需要有一个高层次的理解。

如何安装项目

如果用户必须在本地设备上安装项目，您应该包括安装项目的必要步骤和所需的依赖项（如果有）。

如何使用项目

请务必指导用户如何使用该项目。 提供分步说明。 当用户遇到任何困难时，这些说明将成为参考点。

您可以通过使用屏幕截图或剪辑等视觉辅助工具来使说明更具交互性，以在用户遵守它们时显示正在运行的项目和结果的示例。

此外，如果您的项目需要身份验证，那么这是包含凭据的正确部分。

如何为项目做贡献

包括有关其他开发人员如何为您的项目做出贡献的说明，如果您打算与其他开发人员协作或者该项目是否是开源的。

学分

如果您以团队的形式参与该项目，则应列出与该项目一起工作的人员名单。 您可以添加他们的 GitHub 个人资料。

执照

有各种类型的许可证。 您可以选择哪一种取决于您正在创建的项目类型。 该许可证可帮助合作者知道该做什么和不该做什么。

还...

  • 如果您的 README 很长，您可能需要添加一个目录，以便用户轻松导航到不同的部分。 这将使读者更容易轻松地在项目中移动。

  • 保持最新 - 确保您的文件是最新的是一种很好的做法。 确保在必要时对其进行更新。

  • 简明扼要，仅包含所需的详细信息。 您的文档应包含所有其他信息。

  • 您可以使用一些工具为您的项目自动生成自述文件。

享受🖤

参考


一旦暂停，他们将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，themfon 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，他们的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果他们的fon 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记它们：

他们不断发布违反 DEV 社区 👩‍💻👨‍💻 行为准则的内容，因为它具有骚扰性、冒犯性或垃圾信息。

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 themfon 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

The first time I saw the word 'README', I was trying to complete a task. The instruction was to create a README.md file; add some information about the project, and push it to my GitHub. I didn't quite understand its purpose until I had to work on a collaborative project that required deployment.

Coding best practice requires that every repository has a README file.

What is a README File?

A README File is a text file that gives users the basic required information about your project at a glance. It describes your project, states why your project is valuable, what users can do with your project, and how they can use it, among other things. It is usually the first thing people engage with when they check out any project you have on your repository. It needs to be concise.

What to Include in a README File

Project Description:

A properly drafted description includes the following: a. Your project name; b. What your project does; c. Who the target user is; d. How it works; and

e. The technologies you used and why you used them

The project description allows other developers and collaborators to have a high-level understanding of what your project entails.

How to Install the Project

If the user has to install the project on a local device, you should include the necessary steps to install your project and the required dependencies (if any).

How to Use the Project

Be sure to guide users on how to use the project. Provide step-by-step instructions. These instructions will be a reference point when users encounter any difficulty.

You can make the instructions more interactive by using visual aids like screenshots or clips to show examples of the running project and results when users adhere to them.

Also, if your project requires authentication, this is the right section to include the credentials.

How to Contribute to the Project

Include instructions on how other developers can contribute to your project, if you intend to collaborate with other developers or if the project is open-source.

Credits

You should include a list of people you worked with on the project if you worked on the project as a team. You can add their GitHub profiles.

License

There are various types of licenses. The one you can choose depends on the kind of project you are creating. The license helps collaborators know what to do and what not to do.

Also...

  • If your README is very long, you might want to add a table of contents to make it easy for users to navigate to different sections easily. It will make it easier for readers to move around the project with ease.

  • Keep it up-to-date - It is good practice to ensure that your file is up-to-date. Make sure to update it when necessary.

  • Be concise and include only the required details. Your documentation should contain all other information.

  • There are tools you can use to automatically generate a README for your project.

Enjoy 🖤

References

 
🌚 Dark mode is available in Settings.

Souptik Debnath ⚡ - Aug 7

Once unsuspended, themfon will be able to comment and publish posts again.

item.title: How docker will save your time, and why should you learn it immediately. docker 将如何节省您的时间，以及您为什么要立即学习它。 (1/2) (1/2) | How docker will save your time, and why should you learn it immediately. (1/2)
item.description:
什么是 Docker？ 为什么它很受欢迎？
在互联网上的任何地方，您都可以找到有关 docker 与 VM 比较的文章和如下图：

但是我将把 Docker作为一个工具来教你，所以它会更容易理解，而且我们不会过多地剖析它的底层功能（我们只做了一些 😅）。

不要安装新的应用程序！ 只是拉他们！

对于初学者来说，docker 最有趣的地方在于能够如此轻松地拉取不同的工具。
如您所见，我们可以选择任何我们想要的应用程序，实际上我们在 Docker 的世界中称它们为镜像。

什么是图像？

映像是在基本映像上运行的应用程序和命令的集合，此基本映像本身是一个简化的 Linux 发行版。
这个 Linux 没有 GUI，可以是任何发行版，例如 Ubuntu、Alpine、Bullseye 等。

例如，您可以安装几乎任何版本的任何数据库，例如 Postgres、Redis 等，方法是拉取它们的映像并将它们作为容器运行。
您可以同时拥有同一应用程序的多个版本。

什么是容器？

图像本身可以被认为是一个可执行文件，除非你运行它，否则它什么都不是，只要你运行它，就会创建一个容器（进程）。
您可以使用图像运行许多相同的容器。

弄脏 Redis！

让我们动手体验一下Redis，
转到此链接

在这幅图片中，
标签可以假设为不同版本的图像，标签名称可能包括基础图像名称及其版本。

官方镜像是 docker 团队不断制作的著名项目，实际上包括你我在内的每个人都可以制作一个镜像并将其作为公共或私有 docker 镜像推送到 Dockerhub 上。

在右上角，你可以看到docker pull redis命令，这是拉取这个镜像最简单的方法，但是我们可以在那个页面的底部找到更详细的命令。

例如，此命令将拉取redis映像并运行一个名为“my-redis-container”的容器：

 docker run --name my-redis-container -d redis

但是你不能使用这个容器，你应该暴露 Redis 端口。

 docker run --name my-redis-container -p 6379:6379 -d redis

你现在可以通过 localhost:6379 访问 Redis

我还能做什么？

  • 您是 Windows 用户，但您喜欢使用 Linux 而不会遇到 VM 或双启动的麻烦吗？ 运行此命令：
 docker run --name itubu -it ubuntu

这将创建一个名为“itubu”的容器并将您的外壳连接到它的外壳。

您想使用 Tor 网络而不用担心配置 Tor 的麻烦吗？ 运行此命令：

 sudo docker run -p 8118:8118 -p 9050:9050 -d dperson/torproxy

现在您可以使用此端口通过 Tor 网络代理您的请求。

  • 你想使用无头浏览器吗？ 运行此命令
docker run -p 3000:3000 browserless/chrome

你将在 localhost:3000 上有一个 chrome 浏览器
它可能对报废非常有帮助。

  • 我们在😄之前讨论过数据库，还有很多其他用例。

它大多不需要努力

但在大多数情况下，作为初学者，您只需要一行命令或 10 行 Dockerfile，就可以使您的项目变得更好。

使用 Docker 的优势在我添加到本文末尾的视频中进行了解释，我不会介绍它们。



原文:

What is Docker? And why is it popular?
Everywhere on the internet you find articles about how docker is compared with VM and diagrams like this:

But I'm going to teach you Docker just as a tool, so it will be so easier to understand, and we don't dissect it's underlying functionality a lot (we do it just a few 😅).

Don't install new apps! just pull them!

The most interesting thing about docker for a beginner, is the ability to pull different tools so easily.
DockerHub will be like App store!

As you see, we can pick up any app that we want, actually we call them images in the world of Docker.

What is an image?

Image is a collection of applications and commands running on a base image and this base image itself is a simplified Linux distro.
This Linux doesn't have a GUI and could be any distro such as Ubuntu, Alpine, Bullseye, etc.

For example, you can install almost any version of any database such as Postgres, Redis, etc, by pulling their image and run them as a container.
You can have multiple versions of the same application at the same time.

What is a container?

Image itself can be supposed as a executable file, it is nothing unless you run it, anytime you run it, a container (process) will be made.
you can run many same containers using an image.

Getting dirty with Redis!

Let's have a hands-on experience with Redis,
Go to this link

In this picture,
Tags can be assumed as different version of images, tag name may include base image name and its version.

Official images are famous projects that are being made constantly by docker team, actually everyone including you and me, can make an image and push it on Dockerhub as a public or private docker image.

At the top right, you can see docker pull redis command, it's the simplest way to pull this image, but we can find more detailed commands at the bottom of that page.

For example this command will pull the redis image and run a container called "my-redis-container":

docker run --name my-redis-container -d redis

-d is used for detaching the process, it will run this container and leave the shell alone for your future use.

But you're not able to use this container, you should expose the Redis port.

docker run --name my-redis-container -p 6379:6379 -d redis

-p 6379:6379 is a port mapping that maps port 6379 of the container to the 6379 of our localhost.
And you can now access Redis through localhost:6379

What other things can I do?

  • Are you a windows user but you like to use Linux without getting into the trouble of VM or Double boot? Run this command: 
docker run --name itubu -it ubuntu

This will make a container named "itubu" and connect your shell to its shell.

the "run" command, first look in your local to see if there is any image called "ubuntu", then if it doesn't exists, docker will pull it from Dockerhub.

  • Do you want to use Tor network without getting into the trouble of configuring tor? Run this command: 
sudo docker run -p 8118:8118 -p 9050:9050 -d dperson/torproxy

dperson/torproxy is an unofficial docker image.

-p 8118:8118 (and -p 9050:9050) is a port mapping that maps port 8118 of container to the 8118 of our localhost.

Now you can proxy your requests through Tor network using this ports.

  • Do you want to use a headless browser? Run this command 
docker run -p 3000:3000 browserless/chrome

And you'll have a chrome browser on localhost:3000
It could be so helpful for scrapping.

  • We spoke about databases before 😄 and there are so many other usecases.

It mostly needs no effort

Docker itself plays an important role in DevOps world, and without a doubt, it has so many tips and tricks. But in most cases, as a beginner, you will just need a one line command or a 10 line Dockerfile, to make your project way much better.

The advantages of using Docker is explained in a video I added to the end of this article and I'm not covering them.
item.author: arshamalh

item.title: 如果我们有一个网站，您需要解决其他开发人员面临的重复生产错误怎么办？ | What if we had a site where you are required to solve duplicated production bugs that other developers have faced?
item.description:
帕特里克·温多

发表于我们都知道 Leetcode 和 Codewars 以及它们如何训练开发人员解决抽象问题，但是我还没有遇到一个网站可以为您提供生产错误供您解决。 我发现这对于学习如何识别和解决问题会更有帮助。 就个人而言，我认为在面试中使用它们会更好。 你怎么看？ 已经有这样的项目了吗？ 与班级分享。

举个例子，你有一个 Rails 应用程序，它需要 2 秒来加载一个页面，并要求你加快速度。
或者 API 端点返回未定义的节点应用程序，您需要找出原因。

我认为通过这种方式，您可以看到问题的不同解决方案，如果它是优化解决方案，您甚至可以在有人无法在本地复制问题时参考该问题。

编码美人鱼🧜‍♀️ - 克里斯蒂安·马加良斯 - 一旦暂停，w3ndo 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，w3ndo 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，w3ndo 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 w3ndo 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 w3ndo 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 w3ndo 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Patrick Wendo

Posted on Sep 1

We know all about Leetcode and Codewars and how these tend to train developers in abstract problem solving, but I haven't met a website that presents you with a production bug for you to solve. I find that this would be a lot more beneficial to learn how to identify and solve problems. Personally, I think it would be even better to use them in interviews. What do you think? And is there already a project like this? Share with the class.

An example would be you're given a rails application that takes 2s to load up a page and you're asked to speed it up.
Or an node application where an API endpoint is returning undefined and you're required to figure out why.

I think this way you can see different solutions to your problem, if it's an optimization one, and you can even reference the problem when someone can't duplicate it locally.

The Coding Mermaid 🧜‍♀️ - Aug 15

Cristian Magalhães - Jul 26

Once suspended, w3ndo will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, w3ndo will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by w3ndo will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If w3ndo is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Thanks for keeping DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻 safe. Here is what you can do to flag w3ndo:

w3ndo consistently posts content that violates DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging w3ndo will restore default visibility to their posts.

德夫库斯塔夫

  1. transform: translate(-50%, -50%); 用于使表格居中

原文:

devkoustav

Posted on Sep 1

What will we come up with? 💚

Let's create it🎯🔥

Body Structure

HTML 

<form>

    <h2>Login Here</h2>

    <label for="username">Username</label>
    <input type="text" placeholder="Enter your User " id="username">
    <label for="password">Password</label>
    <input type="password" placeholder="Enter a 8+ character password" id="password">

    <button>Log In</button>

    <div class="sociallogin">
      <div class="go"><b class="fab fa-google"></b> Google</div>
      <div class="fb"><b class="fab fa-facebook"></b> Facebook</div>
    </div>

  </form>

📢 Tip💡
If you want to put the * mark to show required

use -

CSS

Some fixed values.

* {
   padding: 0;
   margin: 0;
   box-sizing: border-box;
}

Styling the body....🔥

body {
    background-image: url("https://dev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com/uploads/articles/0bo50etkr3xgjgjcp4z8.jpg");
    background-size: cover;
    background-repeat: no-repeat;
    font-family: 'Nunito', sans-serif;
  }

📢 Tip 💡

To make the form look cool💚 use-

::selection {
    color: #003300;
    background-color: #e6ffe6;
  }

By the way I have covered this ::selection in Day 2. If you haven't read it yet...give it a look...!

Let's style the form..

form {
    height: 520px;
    width: 400px;
    position: absolute;
    transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
    top: 50%;
    left: 50%;
    border-radius: 10px;
    backdrop-filter: blur(10px);
    border: 2px solid rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.1);
    box-shadow: 0 0 40px rgba(8, 7, 16, 0.6);
    padding: 50px 35px;
  }

  form * {
    color: #ffffff;
    letter-spacing: 0.5px;
    outline: none;
    border: none;
  }
  1. transform: translate(-50%, -50%); is used to bring the form in center
  2. blur is used to give the frosted glass effect over the background image.

Let's style Login Here

form h2 {
    font-size: 32px;
    font-weight: 500;
    line-height: 42px;
    text-align: center;
    font-family: 'Playfair Display', serif;
  }

Now comes the label

label {
    display: block;
    margin-top: 30px;
    font-size: 16px;
    font-weight: 500;
  }

Now input💚

input {
    display: block;
    height: 50px;
    width: 100%;
    background-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.07);
    border-radius: 3px;
    padding: 0 10px;
    margin-top: 8px;
    font-size: 14px;
    font-weight: 300;
  }

🎯 Tip 💡

Use the :focus for cool look...

input:focus {
    border-style: solid;
    border-color: rgb(0, 179, 0);
    border-width: 1px;
    box-shadow: 0 4px 8px 0 rgb(0, 179, 0, 0.3),
      0 6px 20px 0 rgb(0, 179, 0, 0.2);
  }

The placeholder

::placeholder {
   color: #e5e5e5;
 }

Here's the Log In button

button {
    margin-top: 50px;
    width: 100%;
    background-color: #ffffff;
    color: #080710;
    padding: 15px 0;
    font-size: 18px;
    font-weight: 600;
    border-radius: 5px;
    cursor: pointer;
  }

Styling the social icons - Google & Facebook

.social {
    margin-top: 30px;
    display: flex;
  }
.social .fb{
  margin-left: 25px;
}
.social b{
  margin-right: 4px;
}
.social div {
   background: red;
   width: 150px;
   border-radius: 3px;
   padding: 5px 10px 10px 5px;
   background-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.27);
   color: #eaf0fb;
   text-align: center;
}

🎯 Tip 💡

To give some cool look let's use-

  1. :hover - when mouse is placed over the
  2. :active - when the div is clicked 
. button div:hover {
    background-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.47);
  }
.button div:active {
    background-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.60)
.social div:hover {
    background-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.47);
  }
.social div:active {
    background-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.60)
  }

CONGRATULATIONS✨ WE MADE

A Responsive Login Form

Now it's your turn 🎯

🎯 Task of the Day🔥 1.

Try this

Sign Up Form

2.

Credits
Images - Unsplash
Fonts - Google Fonts

Check out the whole series!
Share it💚 with someone who may benefit from this ❤️ Happy Coding!

Follow for more!

🌚 Enable dark mode🔠 Change your default font

📚 Adjust your experience level to see more relevant contentA

Josue Luzardo Gebrim - Aug 6

item.title: 理想的任务管理应用 | Ideal task management app
item.description:
你好社区👋

您理想的任务管理又名 todo 应用程序是什么？

哪些功能是必需的，哪些不是？

也许是云同步或跨平台应用程序？ 或者能够为您的任务添加子任务或描述？ 也许这都是关于界面和动画的美感？

让我们分享我们最喜欢的应用程序以及原因！


尼克 | 一旦暂停，ytskk 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，ytskk 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，ytskk 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 ytskk 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 ytskk 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 ytskk 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Hello community 👋

What is your ideal task management aka todo app?

Which features are required and which are not?

Maybe cloud sync or cross platform app? Or ability to add subs tasks or description to your tasks? Maybe it's all about the beauty of the interface and animations?

Let’s share our favorite apps and why!

 
🌚 Dark mode is available in Settings.

BinalPrajapati123 - Aug 30

Nick | BetterWays.dev - Aug 29

Once suspended, ytskk will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, ytskk will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by ytskk will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If ytskk is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Unflagging ytskk will restore default visibility to their posts.
